2604.23552.title: On the Memorization of Consistency Distillation for Diffusion Models
2604.23552.paper_summary: Diffusion models are central to modern generative modeling, and understanding how they balance memorization and generalization is critical for reliable deployment. Recent work has shown that memorization in diffusion models is shaped by training dynamics, with generalization and memorization emerging at different stages of training. However, deployed diffusion models are often further distilled, introducing an additional training phase whose impact on memorization is not well understood. In this work, we analyze how distillation reshapes memorization behavior in diffusion models, taking consistency distillation as a representative framework. Empirically, we show that when applied to a teacher model that has memorized data, consistency distillation significantly reduces transferred memorization in the student while preserving, and sometimes improving, sample quality. To explain this behavior, we provide a theoretical analysis using a random feature neural network model [Bonnaire et al., 2025], showing that consistency distillation suppresses unstable feature directions associated with memorization while preserving stable, generalizable modes. Our findings suggest that distillation can serve not only as an acceleration tool, but also as a mechanism for improving the memorization-generalization trade-off.
2604.23552.paper_summary_zh: 擴散模型是現代生成建模的核心，理解它們如何平衡記憶與泛化對於可靠的部署至關重要。最近的研究顯示，擴散模型中的記憶是由訓練動態所塑造的，泛化和記憶在訓練的不同階段出現。然而，已部署的擴散模型通常會進一步進行蒸餾，這引入了一個額外的訓練階段，其對記憶的影響尚不清楚。在本研究中，我們分析了蒸餾如何重塑擴散模型中的記憶行為，並將一致性蒸餾作為代表性框架。實證上，我們顯示當應用於已經記住數據的教師模型時，一致性蒸餾顯著減少了學生模型中的轉移記憶，同時保持，並有時改善樣本質量。為了解釋這種行為，我們提供了一個理論分析，使用隨機特徵神經網絡模型 [Bonnaire et al., 2025]，顯示一致性蒸餾抑制了與記憶相關的不穩定特徵方向，同時保留了穩定的、可泛化的模式。我們的發現表明，蒸餾不僅可以作為加速工具，還可以作為改善記憶與泛化權衡的機制。
2604.23552.author: Bingqing Jiang et.al.
